title: drymaria engleriana var. devia
intro: Drymaria engleriana var. devia, also known as creeping drymaria, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, creeping drymaria can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
